content.full_text:
March 5, 1991

Kevin Braaten-Moen
Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 610
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Braaten-Moen:

This letter is to request written confirmation of our telephone conversation last February regarding the administration of Supervisor Carole Migden's campaign committees.

My understanding is that, under current law, only one checking account is allowed per election.

Therefore, since Supervisor Migden already has a checking account for her campaign committee -- Carole Migden for Supervisor 1990 -- she is not allowed to have a separate checking account for her supervisor officeholder committee -- Friends of Carole Migden.

If this understanding is correct, I need to know as soon as possible. I have asked our City Attorney for advice on how to report campaign expenses and officeholder expenses if we can only have one checking account. The problem, as you know, is that San Francisco has a $500 limit on campaign contributions but no limit on officeholder contributions.

Thank you for your timely assistance on this matter.

Sincerely yours,

[Signature]
Paul Herman
Legislative Assistant

0179w

cc: Don Disler
     Randy Riddle, City Attorney
